prompt:
Address Gemini code review comments on PR #9 (Telegram chat backend). There are 5 issues to fix:

1. CRITICAL - Data race on chatID field (internal/chat/telegram.go:34): The chatID field is accessed by multiple goroutines without synchronization. handleUpdate writes to it from the Listen goroutine, while Send and SendThread read from other goroutines. Fix by using sync/atomic: atomic.CompareAndSwapInt64 for writes in handleUpdate, atomic.LoadInt64 for reads in Send/SendThread.

2. HIGH - Unhandled error from t.bot.Send(reply) (internal/chat/telegram.go:187): When sending the agent response to the user, the error is silently dropped. Log it with t.logger.Error.

3. MEDIUM - Backend switch uses default for googlechat (cmd/reel-life/main.go:77): If a user provides an invalid backend name, it falls through to googlechat setup. Fix: make googlechat an explicit case and add a default case that reports an error for unsupported backends.

4. MEDIUM - Unhandled errors from API calls (internal/chat/telegram.go:160,176): Errors from sending help message (line 160) and typing indicator (line 176) are silently dropped. Log them appropriately (Error for help message, Warn for typing indicator).

After fixing, run go build ./... and go test ./... to verify everything passes.
